meta.title: [4K] Watch the last Starship Version 2 launch // #Flight11
meta.description:
SpaceX is launching the 11th full stack Starship with its Super Heavy booster, B15.2. This previously flown booster will again not be caught as SpaceX will attempt some more hazardous engine out scenarios with the booster, so it will splashdown off the coast.

Following the success of flight 10, Starship S38 will further test the limits of the ship and heatshield, and hopefully propulsively splashdown in the Indian Ocean.

00:00 - Intro
02:14 - Prelaunch Preview
15:15 - Q&A
01:48:30 - Prop Load
02:09:55 - SpaceX Stream
02:37:51 - Liftoff!
02:40:50 - Stage Sep
02:44:10 - Booster Landing
02:46:50 - SECO
02:54:39 - Starlink Deploy
03:15:20 - Raptor Relight
03:16:40 - Coast Phase
03:20:00 - Reentry
03:41:30 - Belly Flop
03:44:16 - Starship Landing
03:47:40 - Q&A

Want to know what's new with Starship Version 2? We've got a video for you! - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eQZbi8HBRcQ

Want more information on how exactly they'll catch Super Heavy? WATCH THIS - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pAPt5vbr-YU

Want to know where to watch this live? I made a video on how to visit Starbase and where to watch a launch from - https://youtu.be/aWvHrih-Juk

Learn more about Everyday Astronaut Mission Control by Guinn Partners! - http://guinnpartners.com
